usage: setIntegRouteConstraint [-help] -type {bus diffPair match NDR netClass shield simpleNet additional} {-net {<net1 net2 ...>} | -busSinkGroups <string> | -file <string>} [[-file <string>] | [[-name <string>] [-rule <NDR name>] [-shieldNet {<net1 net2>}] [-shieldType {unspecified side bottom bottomSide top topSide topBottom coaxial tandemSplit interleaved crosstie tandemSplitFull tandemSplitFullCoaxial tandemSplitNearBy tandemSplitSpecial none}] [-shieldGap {<layer1 gap1> <layer2[:layerN] gap2> ...}] [-shieldWidth {<layer1 width1> <layer2[:layerN] width2>...}] [-tandemWidth {<layer1 width1> <layer2[:layerN] width2>...}] [-tandemCapWidth {<layer1 width1> <layer2[:layerN] width2>...}] [-tandemOverhang {<layer1 width1> <layer2[:layerN] width2>...}] [-tandemSideOverhang {<layer1 width1> <layer2[:layerN] width2>...}] [-tandemAggressorAdjacentLayerSeparation {number_of_tracks}] [-tandemAggressorSameLayerTrackDistance {<layer1 n1> <layer2[:layerN] n2>...}] [-tandemNearbyNonAggressorType {typeNameString}] [-tandemNearbyNonAggressorExceptByName {nameString}] [-tandemNearbyNonAggressorNets {nameString}] [-groupToOutsideSpacing {<layer1 spacing1> <layer2[:layerN] spacing2>...}] [-busGroupWidth <integer>] [-hierarchicalScope {local local_below local_above local_above_below}] [-layerGap {<layer1 gap1> <layer2[:layerN] gap2>...}] [-matchGap {<layer1 gap1> <layer2[:layerN] gap2>...}] [-tolerance <float>] [-shieldTolerance {<net1 value1> <net2 value2> ...}] [-minRes <float>] [-maxRes <float>] [-matchStyle {accordion trombone coil}] [-topLayer <string>] [-bottomLayer <string>] [-connectSupply {float anyPoint}] [-layerMatch ] [-hard ] [-noshareshield ] [-minLength <float>] [-maxLength <float>] [-cornerStyle {none crossover river}] [-minBusBit <integer>] [-intergroupGap {<layer1 spacing1> <layer2[:layerN] spacing2>...}] [-accessStyle {none straight clustered}] [-busSinkGroups <string>] [-crossTieShieldInterval <string>] [-crossTieShieldedLayers <string>] [-crossTieShieldLayerLimit <string>] [-accessStyle {none straight clustered}] [-matchType {length resistance maxResistance}] [-bus_bit_order ]]]

options.name: -help
options.description: Prints out the command usage

options.name: -accessStyle
options.description: Indicates the preferred style for routing the bus after connecting to the bus pins.

options.name: -bottomLayer
options.description: Indicates the bottom-most routing layer to use

options.name: -busGroupWidth
options.description: specifies the width of the busgroup, to be split in, integer.

options.name: -busSinkGroups
options.description: List of busSinkGroup elements

options.name: -bus_bit_order
options.description: Route the nets in the same order as provided with -nets option in the bus constraint.

options.name: -connectSupply
options.description: Indicates how the shield wires will be connected to the supply line. Default=anyPoint.

options.name: -cornerStyle
options.description: Indicates the style of diffpair route when changing direction.

options.name: -crossTieShieldInterval
options.description: {integer | {<layer1 interval1> <layer2[:layerN] interval2>...}} The number of tracks between the crosstie wires for opportunistic coaxial shielding. 0 = all tracks, 1 = one track between, 2 = two tracks between, ..{N} This attribute is optional and the default value is 0

options.name: -crossTieShieldLayerLimit
options.description: {layer_number:layer_number} The layer range that will be used as cross tie shield net

options.name: -crossTieShieldedLayers
options.description: {layer:layer} The layer range that will get shielding above and below

options.name: -file
options.description: specifies the additionalConstraint file path.

options.name: -groupToOutsideSpacing
options.description: {<layer1 spacing1> <layer2[:layerN] spacing2>...} specifies the spacing per layer(in micron) between nets of this group with the other nets in the design

options.name: -hard
options.description: Specifies whether the created topLayer/bottomLayer constraint would be hard/soft type. Default is soft.

options.name: -hierarchicalScope
options.description: {local local_below local_above local_above_below} specifies whether a particular constraint is local, or can be pushed(below) or pulled(above) across the hierarchy. Default=local

options.name: -intergroupGap
options.description: {<layer1 spacing1> <layer2[:layerN] spacing2>...} Specifies the gap between the split busgroups.

options.name: -layerGap
options.description: {<layer1 gap1> <layer2[:layerN] gap2>...} Indicates the layer names and the gap-spacing values in micron for the nets routed as diffPair. For any layer not listed, the minimum legal spacing for that layer is used

options.name: -layerMatch
options.description: Specifies whether each separate layer should be matched for match nets. By default only the total length is matched

options.name: -matchGap
options.description: {<layer1 gap1> <layer2[:layerN] gap2>...} Indicates the spacing between the two rises of accordion. This applied only when type is match and matchStyle is accordion.

options.name: -matchStyle
options.description: Indicates the routing topology to be used for matching the nets routed as match. Default=accordion

options.name: -matchType
options.description: {length resistance maxResistance} Indicates the topology to be used for matching the nets routed as match.

options.name: -maxLength
options.description: Indicates the maximum length the router should route a net.

options.name: -maxRes
options.description: Indicates the maximum resistance to be used for the specific net being routed, or for the nets with match constraint if -type is match.

options.name: -minBusBit
options.description: Indicates the minimum bits allowed for the router to split a bus

options.name: -minLength
options.description: Indicates the the minimum length the router should route a net.

options.name: -minRes
options.description: Indicates the minimum resistance to be used for the specific net being routed, or for the nets with match constraint if -type is match.

options.name: -name
options.description: indicates the name of the constraint group being created, changing the default name given to the constraint by Innovus

options.name: -net
options.description: List of net names to be routed

options.name: -noshareshield
options.description: Specifies whether there will be common or individual shields between nets in bus constraint.

options.name: -rule
options.description: The routing rule to be used for routing the particular type of net, default is "default"

options.name: -shieldGap
options.description: {<layer1 gap1> <layer2[:layerN] gap2> ...} specifies the spacing(in micron) between the shielded wire and the shield net. For any layer not listed, the minimum legal spacing for that layer is used

options.name: -shieldNet
options.description: Indicates the netGroup should be shielded by nets with given names.

options.name: -shieldTolerance
options.description: {<net1 value1> <net2 value2> ...} Indicates the percentage of length of the net that can remain unshielded, default=20.0

options.name: -shieldType
options.description: {unspecified side bottom bottomSide top topSide topBottom coaxial tandemSplit interleaved crosstie tandemSplitFull tandemSplitFullCoaxial tandemSplitNearBy tandemSplitSpecial none} Used to specify the type of shielding for the net(s) being routed

options.name: -shieldWidth
options.description: {<layer1 width1> <layer2[:layerN] width2>...} specifies the width(in micron) of the shield wire per layer for side shielding. For any layer not listed, the minimum legal width for that layer is used

options.name: -tandemAggressorAdjacentLayerSeparation
options.description: {number_of_tracks} specifies the minimum distance between the given signal layer and the above/below layers where the aggressors are.

options.name: -tandemAggressorSameLayerTrackDistance
options.description: {<layer1 n1> <layer2[:layerN] n2>...} specifies the minimum pitch tracks (distance) between the given signal net and any aggressors on the same layer.

options.name: -tandemCapWidth
options.description: {<layer1 width1> <layer2[:layerN] width2>...} specifies the wire width(in micron) of tandem cap shield beyond the edge of the route being shielded. For any layer not listed, the minimum legal width for that layer is used

options.name: -tandemNearbyNonAggressorExceptByName
options.description: {nameString} specifies that all nets, except the ones mentioned, are not considered as aggressors for shielding.

options.name: -tandemNearbyNonAggressorNets
options.description: {nameString} specifies the net names that are not considered as aggressors for shielding.

options.name: -tandemNearbyNonAggressorType
options.description: {typeNameString} specifies those signal types that are not considered as signal types of aggressors. Even if they exist within a certain range, the signal net does not need to be protected by shielding.

options.name: -tandemOverhang
options.description: {<layer1 width1> <layer2[:layerN] width2>...} specifies the overhang value (in micron) tandem shield.

options.name: -tandemSideOverhang
options.description: {<layer1 width1> <layer2[:layerN] width2>...} specifies the overhang value (in micron) tandem side shield.

options.name: -tandemWidth
options.description: {<layer1 width1> <layer2[:layerN] width2>...} specifies the width(in micron) of the top and bottom shields, beyond the edge of the route being shielded. For any layer not listed, the minimum legal width for that layer is used

options.name: -tolerance
options.description: Indicates the percentage difference in length that is allowed when routing nets as match, default=20.0

options.name: -topLayer
options.description: Indicates the top-most routing layer to use

options.name: -type
options.description: {bus diffPair match NDR netClass shield simpleNet additional} Indicates the type of routing constraints to be created
